title: Nuclear Engineer — Neutron Transport Simulation
about: ASPL Fusion is working to establish accelerator-based neutron-source capability in India for Boron Neutron Capture Therapy (BNCT) and medical isotope production. The neutron transport simulation role is central to accelerator target design and shielding optimisation.
description: Design, execute, and validate Monte Carlo neutron/photon transport simulations for ASPL's accelerator-driven neutron source. You will own the simulation pipeline from geometry modelling through to dose calculations, working directly with the accelerator physics and medical physics teams.
responsibilities: Build and validate Monte Carlo models (OpenMC/MCNP/PHITS/FLUKA) of the accelerator target, moderator assembly, and beam shaping assembly
Perform neutron spectrum optimisation for BNCT epithermal beam quality
Design radiation shielding for the treatment room and facility
Collaborate with mechanical engineering on target thermal-hydraulic coupling
Develop automated simulation workflows and post-processing pipelines
Support regulatory submissions with simulation-backed safety analyses
requirements: M.Sc. or Ph.D. in Nuclear Engineering, Physics, or related field
Proficiency in at least one Monte Carlo code (OpenMC, MCNP, PHITS, or FLUKA)
Strong foundation in neutron physics, cross-section libraries, and transport theory
Programming skills in Python; familiarity with HPC clusters
2+ years of experience in radiation transport simulation (academic or industry)
preferred: Experience with BNCT beam design or medical accelerator shielding
Familiarity with CAD-to-geometry conversion tools (e.g., DAGMC)
Publications in nuclear science or medical physics journals
benefits: Co-authorship on research papers and patents
Direct mentorship from IPR and BARC alumni
